programme.title: Suitcase Killer Murder Trial
programme.sub-title: Extracting and Solidifying the Hard Evidence
programme.desc: Jurors listen to more expert testimony in the field of forensic science.

programme.title: Suitcase Killer Murder Trial
programme.desc: The police trace the movements of Melanie McGuire on the day her husband, Bill, went missing.

programme.title: Treadmill Abuse Murder Trial
programme.desc: The State in their case for murder against the defendant, Christopher Gregor brought to the stand the responding officer who arrived at his apartment. It was after six-year-old Corey Micciolo was pronounced dead at the hospital. Local police decided to search the defendant's home to photograph clues and collect key physical evidence. Corey's initial autopsy concluded he died of a homicide.

programme.title: Treadmill Abuse Murder Trial
programme.desc: The autopsy photos of Corey Micciolo were shown in detail to jurors. The State wanted to present how child abuse and child endangerment by his father, Christopher Gregor, physically led to his death. The defendant's trash became key evidence used against him for the murder of his son. Police retrieved the child's clothing from the kitchen garbage can. It was Corey's sweatshirt, blue jeans and socks soiled with vomit and feces.

programme.title: Treadmill Abuse Murder Trial
programme.desc: Cross-Examination began on the crime scene Investigator Who processed Christopher Gregor's apartment. Detectives immediately turned their attention there when Corey Micciolo was pronounced dead at the hospital and his father ran out of town. Did crime scene investigators miss any important clues? What evidence showed child abuse of this 6-year-old? These were the questions under cross-examination for the CSI supervisor overseeing the crime scene.

programme.title: Treadmill Abuse Murder Trial
programme.desc: The triage nurse who assessed Corey Micciolo shortly before he died testified. This nurse observed Corey's poor, life-threatening condition in the emergency room. He remembered Corey's last breaths recalling how traumatic it was to witness. He noted: "There wasn't a lot of spontaneous movement from Corey. The child was not really moving on his own." It was the defendant, Christopher Gregor, who brought his ailing six-year-old son to the hospital after he was vomiting and appearing lethargic.

programme.title: Killer Girlfriend Murder Trial
programme.sub-title: The Drama That Led to Murder
programme.desc: Ezra McCandless's adopted father continues his testimony, but his answers to questions defends Ezra.

programme.desc: New Jersey millionaire Frank Black flew to West Palm Beach, Florida to close a major business deal but never returned home. When the FBI was called in, agents learned of a mystery woman whom Black met while on his trip.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the early 1980s, a pair of serial bank robbers took hostages and emptied bank vaults across the Midwest.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In July 1996, two men entered a pawnshop in Birmingham, Alabama and callously murdered the employees.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A pair of convicted felons escaped the custody of a local deputy and fled across the country in a wave of abductions and car thefts. When a foreign military officer disappeared along the trail, the FBI believed the pair were growing more violent.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1992, a young mother was gunned down in front of her children and police were left baffled. The crime seemed unmotivated and random. But family members hinted that the victim, who had been married to a prominent attorney, harbored a dark secret.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Violence shattered a quiet Texas neighborhood in 1988 when a mother and child are kidnapped and murdered along with a bystander. In their search for drug trafficker and killer Genaro Ruiz Camacho, the FBI exposed a drug cartel that flourished.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A teenager was shot to death on a hunting ground in Virginia. His death was initially considered an accident but investigators soon suspected foul play. Years later, a man contacted the FBI with a complex story of conspiracy and murder-for-hire.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1996, a group of political extremists robbed a bank in Washington using homemade bombs and military precision to escape. The FBI learned that the robbers might be preparing for a small-scale war with law enforcement.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: For two couples on vacation, an island paradise turned to hell when one couple murders the other. Eleven years after the murder, a drifter sailing the high seas to escape a drug charge was convicted of the crime.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In New York, an armored car robbery left one guard dead and another critically wounded. The FBI suspected domestic terrorists with a history of violent crime. Their investigation led them to the Black Liberation Army and the Weather Underground.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1980, a maid discovered that a copy machine, placed in a busy Las Vegas casino, was actually a bomb.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Gloria, a devoted mother, tried to stop her son David's forbidden relationship. Her attempt to protect her family ended in tragedy as David's hidden violent streak erupted with devastating consequences.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Behind a polite, intelligent exterior, Michael hid violent obsessions. He attacked young women whenever the urge struck, becoming one of Connecticut's most prolific killers until investigators finally stopped him.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of the killer who attacked his ex-girlfriend in a jealous frenzy and committed a brutal murder.

programme.desc: Maria's marriage crumbled when Malcolm discovered her bigamy. As he moved on and found new love, Maria's jealousy erupted into dangerous rage, leading to a confrontation that exposed how far she was willing to go.

programme.desc: In 1985, 121 people in South Dakota and Minnesota were struck by a mysterious illness. There had been only one outbreak like it, and when it happened again no one could figure out why.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A 13-year-old girl went missing from her Colorado home and the only evidence the kidnapper left behind was three fingerprints on a window screen.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A mother of two young children was found dead in her bedroom. Her death was ruled a suicide - but when investigators learned she had almost died in a house fire three years earlier, they decided to take another look at the evidence.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The 1996 investigation into 48-year-old Martha Hansen's murder is helped by a legal requirement that bars have video cameras installed on the premises.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A brilliant young architect became ill and died just before she was to testify in a criminal trial. The autopsy revealed she'd been poisoned with arsenic. Investigators had to determine which person who knew her had a motive for murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a wealthy real estate tycoon goes missing, it appears to be foul play. In his will, he leaves instructions in case he should die under violent circumstances - which were carried out after a hiker comes across his bullet-ridden skull.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a 62-year-old man opens a car restoration business and becomes seriously ill, his doctors cannot find the cause, but forensics can; identification of a highly toxic metal in the victim's blood leads to his poisoner.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police arrested a suspect for the death of a girl. But new technology proved the wrong man was behind bars.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2008, the body of Colorado real estate developer Alan Helmick was found by his wife, Miriam, who then became the prime suspect.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A fireman died from what appeared to be heart disease, but his family was skeptical of the cause of death.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A murder investigation in Florida crosses jurisdictions from New York and Jamaica. The police rely on cell phone mapping, wiretapping and a host of forensic evidence to link a suspect to a murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A cop is found dead. His wife first says the motive was robbery; then she tells police it was suicide.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a dedicated, well-respected teacher disappeared, police had to determine if she'd gone on vacation without telling anyone, or if she was the victim of foul play.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A husband confesses to his wife's murder, only to be found not guilty a year later when a phone tip and forensic evidence point to another.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An investigation into the murder of a World War II veteran slowed to a halt when the prime suspect had a solid alibi. But a lucky break led to a shady character who wore distinctive boots.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When Rosemary Anderson is found dead on the side of the road, her boyfriend becomes the prime suspect. He eventually confessed to her murder, but so did another man - serial killer, Eric Edgar Cooke.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When the mail-order bride of a former army sergeant goes missing, investigators look into the deaths of the man's previous two wives.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A healthy young man dies mysteriously in his sleep, and toxicology tests reveal a lethal dose of lidocaine in his blood.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Rachael Mullenix, 19, was convicted of stabbing her mother and then packing her bloodied corpse in a box.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When an off-duty policeman was shot dead, his fellow officers were determined to solve the crime. They needed clues to find the killer and they discovered them in tiny fibres and an asthma inhaler.

programme.title: Forensic Files
